summary: Recent research in small UAS focuses on enhancing detection, tracking, and security capabilities while addressing operational challenges. One paper introduces a lightweight neural network architecture optimized for real-time detection of small UAVs, tackling issues of size, speed, and visual contrast. Another proposes a heterogeneous observer-follower framework for high-resolution UAV tracking on embedded platforms, resolving the trade-off between image resolution and processing speed. A study explores visual cooperative tracking for open-path gas measurements, integrating drones with Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy for environmental monitoring. Additionally, a multi-physics simulation framework is developed for designing high-power microwave counter-UAS systems, offering a non-kinetic neutralization approach. Finally, a breach-resilient authenticated encryption framework is designed for resource-constrained IoT devices, including small drones, to secure telemetry transmission under adversarial conditions.
